title: American Sovereign Wealth Fund Exploration Act
short_title: American Sovereign Wealth Fund Exploration Act
plain_summary: This bill would create a group of 25 people to study whether the United States should start a special government investment fund. The government would use this fund to buy stocks and other investments to try to make money, just like some other countries do. The study group would spend two years learning about how this would work, where the money would come from, and what effects it might have. After two years, they would tell Congress what they think about whether the US should create this investment fund.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Financial Services.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the American Sovereign Wealth Fund Exploration Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Commission on Exploring the Creation of a Sovereign Wealth Fund of the United States
sections.text:
Within ninety days after this law is passed, the head of the Federal Reserve (the central banking system of the United States) must create a special group called the Commission on Exploring the Creation of a Sovereign Wealth Fund of the United States. A sovereign wealth fund is a large investment account owned by a country that invests money to make more money for the government. This commission will study whether the United States should create its own sovereign wealth fund and make suggestions to Congress about it.

The commission will have twenty five members total. Six members will come from the Federal Reserve System and be chosen by the head of the Federal Reserve. Three members will come from the Treasury Department and be chosen by the Treasury Secretary. Three members will come from the Securities and Exchange Commission (the agency that oversees stock markets) and be chosen by its leader. Two members will come from the Commerce Department and be chosen by the Commerce Secretary. One member will come from the Office of the United States Trade Representative and be chosen by that official. Ten members will be professors or experts who know about economics, money policy, investment policy, social policy, industrial policy, or sovereign wealth funds, and they will be chosen by the head of the Federal Reserve.

Each member serves for two years and can be chosen again for another two year term. If a member leaves early, the same person who chose them originally must pick a replacement within thirty days.

The commission must meet at least once every three months. They must have their first meeting within sixty days after all members are chosen. The commission can create smaller working groups that meet as needed. More than half of the members must be present to make official decisions. The members will choose one of themselves to be the chairperson or leader. If the chairperson leaves, they must choose a new one within thirty days.

The commission must do a thorough investigation that looks at many things. First, they must study whether it is possible to create and run a sovereign wealth fund for the United States, including where the money would come from. This could include natural resources, things the government already owns, payments for using government property, taxes, tariffs on imports, foreign currency reserves, borrowed money, extra money left over in the budget, donations from private people or other countries, and other ways to make money.

They must also study what kinds of investments the fund could make. This could include buying stocks and bonds from companies in America and other countries, real estate, infrastructure like roads and bridges, commodities like gold or oil, private company investments, cash reserves, and other investments that could make money or be important for the country's strategy. They need to figure out what limits there should be on what the fund can invest in.

The commission must study how the money earned by the fund could be used. This might include general government income, existing government programs, direct payments to citizens, helping stabilize the economy and currency, public investments, building infrastructure and development projects, social programs, paying down government debt, investing in important technologies and industries, and other uses that help the country.

They must study how the fund would be managed and operated based on international rules called the Santiago Principles that other countries follow for their sovereign wealth funds. This includes how it would be governed and staffed, what laws would apply, how to keep it independent from politics, how to oversee it and keep it accountable and transparent, ethics rules, how ownership rights would work, how investment and withdrawal decisions would be made, how it would work with other government agencies, accounting questions about where and how money would be stored, and how it would handle losses.

The commission must predict what might happen if the fund is created, including how profitable it might be and what other strategic outcomes might occur. They must study how the fund would affect and be affected by the overall government budget, currency value changes, exchange rates, the stock market, wealth inequality, economic growth and gross domestic product, inflation, real estate markets and housing costs, financial stability, political interference, trade protection policies and retaliatory tariffs, corruption, national security, and other relevant issues.

The commission must also ask for opinions from everyone who cares about creating a sovereign wealth fund for the United States. They must evaluate whether any proposals and current arrangements for the issues they identify are good ideas.

The commission has several powers to do its work. It can hold hearings, meet at different times and places, take testimony from witnesses, and gather evidence. Government agencies can lend their employees to help the commission if asked and if they are paid back for the cost. The commission can hire temporary experts and consultants. It can use the postal service like other government agencies. It can get office space and administrative help from the General Services Administration at normal government rates. It can make contracts with the federal government, state and local governments, private companies, or individuals to help with its work. The chairperson can hire a staff director and other workers as needed, and they can set pay rates up to a certain government pay level. Commission members can be reimbursed for travel expenses when doing commission work.

No later than two years after the commission's first meeting, it must give a public report to the President, relevant government agencies, and relevant Congressional committees. The report must include what they found in their investigation, recommendations based on what they found including detailed suggestions for new laws, and it can include minority opinions if some members disagree with the main findings.
